appliedUpdates.description: services.yaml now exists in .factory/ - LSP tools (LspDefinitionTool, LspReferencesTool, LspHoverTool, LspDiagnosticsTool, LspRenameTool) were discovered in mcp_lsp.rs but not documented. However, per triage guidelines, this is a documentation gap rather than an operational issue - the tools exist and work. Not applied as it would be a documentation-only change outside scope.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the distinction between MCP tree-sitter integration (external MCP servers) and direct tree-sitter Rust bindings (internal implementation). The feature description for ast-dependency-analysis says 'Use MCP tree-sitter tools' but the implementation uses tree_sitter crate directly. This caused confusion during review.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature ast-dependency-analysis description mentions 'MCP tree-sitter tools' but implementation uses crate::treesitter module with tree_sitter::Parser directly. MCP integration exists separately in swell-tools/src/mcp.rs.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The Rust skill should include guidance on tree-sitter integration patterns, as multiple features in milestone 'ast' required this knowledge which was not covered in the skill.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: ast-dependency-graph and ast-dependency-analysis both needed tree-sitter patterns that weren't in the Rust skill. The skill only covers ownership, async, error handling, but not external library integration patterns.

rejectedObservations.observation: Error handling uses String instead of thiserror::Error
rejectedObservations.reason: Not a systemic issue - dependency_graph.rs uses String error returns which is a stylistic choice. The project doesn't mandate thiserror for all crates. No evidence this causes problems.

rejectedObservations.observation: Tree-sitter language support limitations not documented
rejectedObservations.reason: Language support gaps (TypeScript, JavaScript, Go return None from to_language()) are implementation details that don't affect core functionality. Workers can discover this through tests.
services.yaml is for operational services (daemon, MCP servers). LSP tools are code components, not running services. This is a documentation gap, not an operational issue.
